mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls or stays near the zero bound, the discount rate on equities is compressed and risk appetite rises, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month. The effect weakens if inflation is accelerating sharply or if rates are already rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional returns when FEDFUNDS is flat-to-falling, or if triggered periods show negative average returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals weakening household income and lower earnings expectations, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The signal is strongest when PAYEMS is also deteriorating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of UNRATE-rise trigger events, or if the average triggered return is not below baseline for any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad final demand and housing activity are strengthening, which supports revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The interaction matters because either series alone is noisier than their joint improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if joint up-moves in RSAFS and HOUST do not outperform single-series up-moves for 60d forward SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates after a prior slowdown, it signals factory output recovery and better cyclical earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails when demand indicators are simultaneously weakening.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO surprises are followed by negative or flat 20d SPX returns in a majority of trigger events, or if the sign flips in more than one consecutive 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL is decelerating while FEDFUNDS remains low, real rates and inflation fear decline, supporting equity valuation expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism breaks if inflation re-accelerates alongside rate cuts.
failure_condition: Reject if low-inflation episodes with low FEDFUNDS do not produce above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the joint condition has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS trend with a rising UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is strongest when both measures worsen together.
failure_condition: Reject if combined PAYEMS decline and UNRATE rise fail to predict negative 5d SPX returns, or if more than 55% of triggered events produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in GDPC1 relative to prior quarters signals a broad demand slowdown that should depress equity prices over the next quarter as earnings expectations reset lower. The mechanism fails if the decline is isolated to inventories and not broad-based demand.
failure_condition: Reject if low-GDP-growth quarters are not followed by negative 60d SPX returns more often than chance, or if the average forward return during trigger periods is nonnegative over any 8-quarter rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A coexistence of rising CPIAUCSL and rising FEDFUNDS indicates tightening into inflation, which raises real discount rates and should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should disappear once inflation is falling or policy is already easing.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 25 bps in 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if rate-hike episodes with rising CPIAUCSL do not precede negative 20d SPX returns, or if positive returns dominate in triggered periods across any 3-year rolling window.
